content.full_text:
31 March 1977

Evelyn L. Roberson
City Clerk
South Lake Tahoe
P.O. Box 1210
South Lake Tahoe, CA  95705

Dear Ms. Roberson:

     This letter will confirm the advice you received during our telephone conversation of March 29, 1977. In a letter dated March 8, 1977, you asked if the Council for Logic should file campaign disclosure statements. You stated that the Council for Logic spends large amounts of money for paid advertising to support and oppose local issues but not candidates or ballot measures.

     Based on the information contained in your letter and enclosures as well as your representations during our telephone conversation, the Council for Logic does not need to file campaign disclosure statements because they have not raised or spent money for the purpose of influencing or attempting to influence the action of voters for or against the nomination or election of a candidate or candidates, or the qualification or passage of any measure. (See 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18215 and 18225)

     However, the Council for Logic may have a reporting obligation under Government Code Section 86103(b) which requires lobbying disclosure reports to be filed by any person who directly or indirectly makes payments of $250 or more in value in any month to influence legislative or administrative action. If the Council for Logic has spent $250 or more during any month attempting to influence any elective state official, legislative official or agency official, including urging other persons to enter into direct communication with any elective state official, legislative official or agency official, the Council for Logic must file a disclosure report (Form 650) during the month following each month in which $250 or more was spent.

     If you have additional questions or if I may be of further assistance, please contact me.

Sincerely,'


John C. Greenwood
Program Coordinator

JCG:ct     bcc: Jeanne P.

CITY CLERK
Evelyn L. Robinson

P.O. BOX 1210 - SOUTH LAKE TAHOE, CALIFORNIA 95705 - PHONE (916) 541-2900

CITY OF
SOUTH LAKE TAHOE
CALIFORNIA

March 8, 1977


FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICE COMMISSION
State of California
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California  95804

COUNCIL FOR LOGIC

I have been asked if the Council for Logic should file under
Proposition 9, Fair Political Practice Commission regulation
18215 for corporations and associations (copy enclosed). Enclosed
also is some Council for Logic clippings from the local newspaper.
From time to time Council for Logic expends large amounts of money
in paid advertising for and against local issues but not particular
candidates or ballot issues.

Your cooperation in providing information on this matter would be
appreciated.


[signature]

EVELYN L. ROBERSON - CITY CLERK

jmc

Enclosure

RECEIVED
NOV 4 1975

CONTRIBUTIONS
CITY OF SOUTH LAKE TAHOE, CALIFORNIA

EXAMPLES: REGULATION 18215

1. POLITICAL CLUBS

Political clubs normally receive dues from members. The dues
are generally not received exclusively for a political purpose*
but simply to maintain membership, pay overhead, conduct voter
registration drives. The raising of $500 for these purposes
does not make the political club a committee.

When the political club contributes $500 to a political campaign
or for a political purpose, it is a recipient committee if the
club traditionally contributes to candidates or ballot measure
campaigns. If it has never made contributions for a political
purpose, or if at the time of collecting dues members were not
advised that a portion of the dues would be used for political
purposes, the club will have a filing obligation only after
contributing $5,000.

If a political club makes a special fund appeal to members of
the public generally requesting contributions for a specific
political purpose, when $500 is received through this effort,
the club is a recipient committee because the money is earmarked*
for a political purpose.

2. COMMUNITY ORGANIZATIONS

A local organization called "Taxpayers for Reform" raises money
and makes expenditures to achieve the goal of property tax reform
in their community. It has never received or expended money in
support or opposition to a candidate or ballot measure or for
any other political purpose, nor has it received funds earmarked
for a political purpose.

"Taxpayers for Reform" would become a major donor committee when
it expends $5,000 for a political purpose, attempting to
influence the action of the voters for or against the nomination
or election of a candidate or candidates, or the qualification or
passage of any measure.

3. LABOR ORGANIZATIONS

A labor union receives dues from members. The payment of dues
are made by members for union activities. The union then decides
to contribute to political campaigns. What must they file?

The original payment of dues was not for a political purpose nor
was a portion earmarked for later contribution, but because the
union has a history of making political contributions to candidates
and ballot measure campaigns, the union will become a recipient
committee after contributing $500 for political purposes.

*See regulation for definition of political purpose and earmarked.

If a labor union receives dues from members with a specific
amount earmarked for political purposes, they will become a
recipient committee when $500 in earmarked funds has been
received.

4. CORPORATIONS AND ASSOCIATIONS - Political Action Committees (PACs)

Markham Moters Corporation has encouraged its employees to form
a "good government committee." The committee will be funded by
voluntary contributions from employees for the purpose of making
political contributions to candidates and ballot measure campaigns.

The "good government committee" is a recipient committee when
$500 is received since all money received is earmarked for a
political purpose. All contributions received and expenditures
made must be reported by the committee because it is a committee
formed primarily for political purposes.

********

The State Association of Laboratory Technicians decides to become
politically active and asks all its affiliated members to contri-
bute to a special fund to be administered by the state organization.

The Association has established a PAC and must report as a
recipient committee when $500 is received since all money received
is earmarked for a political purpose. All contributions received
and expenditures made must be reported by the committee. Because it
is a committee formed primarily for political purposes, it must
report all of its receipts and expenditures.


COMMITTEE DEFINITIONS:

Recipient Committee - Receives $500 or more in contributions during
                      a calendar year.

Expenditure Committee - Makes expenditures (other than direct monetary
                        contributions) in support or opposition to
                        candidates or measures, makes non-monetary
                        *(in-kind) contributions of goods or services
                        to candidates or committees, repays loans of
                        candidates or committees, if such expenditures
                        and repayments total $500 or more in a calendar
                        year.

Major Donor Committee - Gives or loans $5,000 or more in a calendar
                        year directly to candidates or committees.

FOR FILING ADMINISTRATIVE REGULATIONS
WITH THE SECRETARY OF STATE
(Pursuant to Government Code Section 11350.1)

Copy below is hereby certified to be a true
and correct copy of regulations adopted, amended, or an order of repeal by:

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
(Agency)

Date of adoption, amendment, or repeal:

April 22, 1976

By: [illegible]
Chairman
(Title)

ENDORSED
APPROVED FOR FILING
(Gov. Code 11352)
APR30 1976

Office of Administrative Hearings

DO NOT WRITE IN THIS SPACE


ORDER ADOPTING REGULATIONS OF THE

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

After proceedings had in accordance with the provisions of the

Administrative Procedure Act (Government Code Title 2, Div. 3, Part

1, Ch. 4.5) and pursuant to the authority vested by Section 83112

of the Government Code, and to implement, interpret or make

specific Section 82015 of the Government Code, the Fair Political

Practices Commission hereby adopts its regulation in Division VI,

Title 2, California Administrative Code, as follows:


Adopt Section 18215:

Chapter 2: Definitions

18215. Contribution (82015)

(a) "Political purposes," as used in Government Code Section

82015, means made for the purpose of influencing or attempting to

influence the action of the voters for or against the nomination or

election of a candidate or candidates, or the qualification or

passage of any measure.

WITH THE SECRETARY OF STATE
(Pursuant to Government Code Section 11320.1)

(b) "Contribution" includes any monetary or non-monetary

payment for which full and adequate consideration is not made to

the donor that is received by or made at the behest of:

(1) A candidate, unless it is clear from surrounding

circumstances that the payment was received or made at his

behest for personal purposes unrelated to his candidacy

or status as an office holder. The term "payment" includes

the candidate's own money or property used on behalf of his

candidacy;

(2) A controlled committee;

(3) An official committee of a political party, includ

a state central committee, county central committee, assembl:

district committee or any subcommittee of such committee; or

(4) An organization formed or existing primarily for

political purposes, including, but not limited to a politica.

action committee established by any membership organization,

labor union or corporation;

(c) "Made at the behest of" means a payment made under the

control or at the direction of a candidate, controlled committee,

official committee of a political party, or organization formed o

existing primarily for political purposes.

(d) "Contribution" includes any monetary or non-monetary

payment for which full and adequate consideration is not made to

the donor that is received by any person or organization other th

a candidate, controlled committee, official committee of a politi

- 2 -

3/31/67 757-T

WITH THE SECRETARY OF STATE

(Pursuant to Government Code Section 11350.1)

party, or organization formed or existing primarily for political

purposes if the payment is "earmarked" for the making of contri-

butions or expenditures. A payment is "earmarked" when, at the

time of making the payment, the donor knows or has reason to know

that the payment or funds with which the payment will be commingled

will be used to make contributions, as defined in Government Code

Section 82015 and this regulation, or expenditures, as defined in

Government Code Section 82025 and 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18225.

If the donor knows or has reason to know that only part of the

payment will be used to make contributions or expenditures, the

payment shall be apportioned on a reasonable basis in order to

determine the amount of the contribution. Factors relevant to a

determination that the donor has reason to know that all or part of

the payment will be used to make expenditures and contributions

include but are not limited to the established practice of the

person or organization with respect to expenditures and contri-

butions and any representations made when the payment is solicited.

(e) Notwithstanding any provision of this section, volunteer

personal services or payments made by a person for his own

travel expenses are not contributions if such payments are made

voluntarily without any understanding or agreement that they will

be repaid.

* * * * *

This regulation will not create any new or increased costs to

local government pursuant to Section 2231 of the Revenue and Taxati

Code.

#1634 7932-2/11 82

Bureaucratic Ballet

(Editor's note: The opinions expressed in the following column are those of the author, and may or may not reflect those of the magazine.)

By TERRY TRUPP

Warning! Residents of the south shore of Lake Tahoe — both taxpayers and those who depend upon the construction industry to survive. Although none of the agencies, with the exception of the TRPA has as yet taken any new action, the following things, we believe, are about to occur.

(1) The CTRPA has recently decided that it must review all applications for construction of any kind. This is the first step toward implementation of their plan to restrict permits to a certain number per year. The last proposal was for 300 units annually, not on a lottery system but on the basis of selections made by CTRPA. There are approximately 15,000 unbuild lots on the South Shore. If 300 were selected in the annual building limit, it could be 20 years before you would be allowed to build on your lot.

(2) In the past, there has been conversion from the Lahontan Water Quality Control Board about the alleged need for a ban on new sewer hookups on the South Shore. We believe at this time that such an action may be implemented very soon, creating a local moratorium on new construction. The most insidious part of this proposal is that there is absolutely nothing being done or being proposed to expand sewer capacity. Conversely, everything possible has been done by these same agencies to frustrate attempts to enlarge our sewer facilities.

We believe that if such a ban is enacted, it proves beyond any shadow of a doubt that sewer capacity has not been restricted intentionally to provide an excuse for enactment to stop all new development. That in itself is the true goal rather than a legitimate attempt to mitigate environmental problems and create guidelines which will ultimately provide for both man and ecological protection.

(3) The TRPA has presently created restrictions on dredging which will close the majority of marinas and boatyards in the Tahoe basin. Although on the surface it would appear that they are attempting to close the lake, the restrictions will negate the ability of marinas to close the channels for boaters. Also under discussion is a new ordinance which will prohibit the use of buoys in the lake except for those wealthy enough to afford lakefront property which touches the water.

In simple terms, this is another case of the TRPA providing for the wealthy the recreational opportunities they desire while at the same time depriving all others of similar opportunities.

Food for Thought: As we have indicated to you in the past, it is the intention of some appointed to the CTRPA and TRPA to first take all possible private property without compensation to owners and, secondly, to destroy the economic health of this community, ultimately resulting in economic chaos and a forced exodus from this area by those who must work for a living, then finally, implementation of the Sierra Club's proposal to eliminate all of man's encroachments in the Tahoe basin.

The contractors, subcontractors and their employees will be the first large group forced to leave — then the suppliers and their employees will be next. Think about it — furniture stores, glass companies, cement companies, masonry suppliers, lumber yards, paint stores, lighting fixture suppliers, electrical suppliers, roofing materials suppliers. How then will all of the retail and wholesale stores secure new customers? From what industry? Who will buy the trucks, cars, clothes and food formerly used by the many who will soon be forced to leave? What industry do we have that will take the place of tourism?

Regional government is soon going to implement its "new" anti-transportation plan, again with two goals — first, to impose a tax on visitors and residents alike as user fees, and secondly, the beginning of a tourist harassment designed to discourage their coming to Tahoe. Already ski resorts have been denied the right to accommodate more skiers. The goal is "no growth" everywhere. Marinas and boating companies are prevented from providing better facilities for the public. The Sierra Club's stated goal is to remove gaming from the Tahoe basin. Because of TRPA and CTRPA, we have no solution to our unsafe, outdated, and clogged highway system. The CTRPA's transportation plan states, "When the congestion gets bad enough, people will go elsewhere."

The limits of this community's economic endurance have not been further increased. Any intrusion by government toward reducing our "tourist oriented economy will ultimately lead to a disaster. We suggest that you begin writing letters to anyone who believes they can help in stopping these non-elected agencies from destroying our future. If you wish to remain here and you are not wealthy enough to retire, it is long past time that you begin to work toward re-establishing some form of commerce sense and responsive elected government.

Let us hear your views — Contact Council for Logic, P.O. Box 6126, South Lake Tahoe, Calif. 95729.

Friday, September 24, 1976 Tahoe Daily Tribune

Bureaucratic Ballet

(Editor's note: The opinions expressed in the following column are those of the author, and may not reflect those of the newspaper.)

By BERRY TRUPP

The following is a letter that will be mailed to property owners in the Tahoe Basin.

Dear Friends and Property Owners:

Let's look back on five years of performance by regional government—two overlapping bureaucracies of TRPA and CTRPA.

REGIONAL GOVERNMENT HAS:

1. Denied us elected representation; we now have APPOINTEES with a programmed philosophy and vote.

2. Taken or prevented use of property without compensation while taxes and assessments march on.

3. Ignored realistic solutions to our traffic problems, based on the philosophy of "when congestion gets bad enough, people will go elsewhere."

4. Tarnished Tahoe's image with untrue statements about "polluted" air and water;

misrepresentation to secure the support of legislators.

5. Designed a playground for the wealthy that will exclude most Americans.

6. Replaced orderly growth with panic building.

7. Caused accelerated taxes and rents, with even greater increases to come.

8. Increased the cost of building a home without increasing its value.

9. Because of excessive regulations, forced condemnation upon us that are not in the tahoe image.

10. Prevented the rebuilding of our blighted areas that could become a source of revenue instead of arbitrary land coverage restrictions or massive downzoning.

11. Devalued property values; many properties are being lost through foreclosures, because owners are denied use—and now cannot sell.

12. Bent totally insensitive to both local government and its citizens.

13. Used to an extreme the premise that the end justifies the means—but what have the means accomplished?

Please think carefully—after hundreds and hundreds of studies, and millions upon millions of dollars spent...WHAT HAS REGIONAL GOVERNMENT DONE FOR THE TAHOE COMMUNITY? IT'S CITIZENS...OR THE 200,000,000 AMERICANS IT IS "SAVING" IT FOR?

WEST AND EAST SHORES OF LAKE TAHOE... are to be designated as a National Recreation Area with the public with a development of 40,000 new campsites. THE TRPA PROPOSALS ARE:

A. That property owners on the West Shore should surrender their homes to state or federal agencies through an amortization process of tax writeoffs when fully depreciated the home belongs to the state.

B. Creation of a life estate whereby the home may (or may not) be purchased, and that current owner could then remain until he died. The property reverts to the state.

Council for Logic urges you to consider these important points:

1. The TRPA compact does not provide authority for eminent domain (i.e. Con-pensation at fair market value when property is condemned).

2. We frequently we have heard or read that West Shore property owners are saying in effect—"Take those South Shore and North Shore properties, but leave ours alone." Many of you earlier supported the TRPA and CTRPA's use of police power (taking or denying use of without compensation). Ultimately when it diminishes the constitutional rights of another, they too become affected by the same action of rights.

3. Council for Logic was formed to protect others and property owners everywhere from the continued use of elected representation to appointed government and against the taking of private property as allowed in a sin against all Americans. Remember all our efforts are on your behalf, too!

Please help by contributing to this cause. Checks should be mailed to COUNCIL FOR LOGIC and mailed to P.O. Box 5123, South Lake Tahoe, California 95729.

sections.question: I have been asked if the Council for Logic should file under Proposition 9, Fair Political Practices Commission regulation 18215 for corporations and associations (copy enclosed). Enclosed also is some Council for Logic clippings from the local newspaper. From time to time Council for Logic expends large amounts of money in paid advertising for and against local issues but not particular candidates or ballot issues.
sections.question_synthetic: Must the Council for Logic organization file as a committee under FPPC regulations when it makes expenditures for paid advertising on local issues but not on specific candidates or ballot measures?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request from a city clerk to FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. The document includes the request letter, newspaper clippings, and FPPC regulatory material (Regulation 18215) as enclosures/supporting documents. No formal FPPC response or conclusion section is present. The regulatory text and examples are included as reference material to the question being asked.

embedding.summary: City Clerk Evelyn L. Roberson requests FPPC guidance on whether the Council for Logic organization must file under Proposition 9 and FPPC Regulation 18215, given that the organization makes substantial expenditures for advertising on local issues. The document includes regulatory guidance on committee definitions and examples of filing obligations for various organizational types.
